jokes.text: Dear God, woman.
jokes.setup: Leslie enthusiastically planning a baby shower with detailed activities
jokes.punchline: Ron's horrified reaction to the baby shower planning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson character moment - his masculine horror at feminine baby shower activities delivered with characteristic deadpan brevity

jokes.text: What are you doing up there in space, baby? Ah! So cute!
jokes.setup: Leslie planning to make astronaut onesie at baby shower
jokes.punchline: Her immediate roleplay with imaginary space baby
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's enthusiastic, childlike approach to everything, but fairly standard character beat

jokes.text: Oh, my God, whose baby is that? That would be mine.
jokes.setup: Everyone suddenly notices Ron has a baby
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact revelation that it's his
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal - Ron casually having his baby at work without telling anyone is perfectly in character and creates genuine surprise

jokes.text: Guys, get in here! Ron has a baby!
jokes.setup: Ron quietly reveals he has a baby
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately announces it to the entire office
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Leslie can't contain information while Ron prefers privacy

jokes.text: John middle name redacted Swanson
jokes.setup: Ron introducing his baby's full name
jokes.punchline: The formal 'redacted' middle name presentation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific to Ron's paranoid, privacy-obsessed character while being formally presented like a government document

jokes.text: John was born some time ago, weighing multiple pounds and several ounces.
jokes.setup: Expectation of specific birth details
jokes.punchline: Deliberately vague, unhelpful information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line - gives technically accurate but completely useless information in the most formal way possible

jokes.text: Much like his father, he is a fan of silence. Please keep your voices down.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his baby's personality
jokes.punchline: Asking the loud office to be quiet for his silent baby
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency - Ron projecting his own preferences onto his newborn and making practical demands

jokes.text: Just let her tire herself out.
jokes.setup: Leslie panicking about not sending Diane a gift
jokes.punchline: Ron treating Leslie like an overexcited pet
jokes.explanation: Ron's practical, detached approach to Leslie's emotional outbursts, treating her like an animal or child

jokes.text: When did you get a fish?
jokes.setup: Ron asking someone to pick up fish food
jokes.punchline: The logical follow-up question that reveals more secrets
jokes.explanation: Good setup for revealing Ron has been hiding multiple aspects of his life, building the running gag

jokes.text: Um, he dislocates his shoulder to the music of Billy Joel.
jokes.setup: April suggesting booking Orin as headliner
jokes.punchline: The disturbing description of Orin's 'performance'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully bizarre and specific - perfectly captures the weird art performance culture and April's deadpan delivery

jokes.text: The Pawnee Journal called it, 'Why would anyone do this?'
jokes.setup: Setting up a newspaper review quote
jokes.punchline: The review is just a confused question instead of criticism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical review quotes - the newspaper is so baffled they can only ask why it exists

jokes.text: described himself as 'Jackson Pollock-esque' and I told him that's finger painting for adults and I hate it!
jokes.setup: Craig explaining why they lost an artist
jokes.punchline: His aggressive dismissal of abstract art
jokes.explanation: Good Craig moment - his intensity applied to art criticism with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Pawnee might once again tolerate-slash-ignore me.
jokes.setup: Leslie talking about political success
jokes.punchline: Her realistic assessment of the best-case scenario
jokes.explanation: Great political humor - Leslie's optimistic personality contrasting with realistic expectations of political approval

jokes.text: I know that sounds bad, but for a politician it's pretty much the best-case scenario.
jokes.setup: Leslie acknowledging her low expectations
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact acceptance of political reality
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire - the cynical reality of politics delivered through Leslie's earnest character

jokes.text: 'Grant Larson,' otherwise known as Elton John.
jokes.setup: Tom confidently identifying Grant Larson
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong identification as Elton John
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom moment - confident ignorance about something outside his wheelhouse

jokes.text: He's basically the Liam Bonneville of the Midwest.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to explain Grant Larson's importance
jokes.punchline: Using an equally obscure reference that doesn't help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - her encyclopedic knowledge of obscure government officials that helps no one

jokes.text: The department of the interior's resident bad boy?
jokes.setup: Ben trying to understand who Liam Bonneville is
jokes.punchline: The oxymoron of a government 'bad boy'
jokes.explanation: Great bureaucratic humor - the absurdity of someone being rebellious within the Department of Interior

jokes.text: He's basically the Tim Dweck-- Okay, never mind.
jokes.setup: Leslie starting another obscure comparison
jokes.punchline: Her giving up when she realizes no one will know this reference either
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Leslie's self-awareness about her obscure references

jokes.text: This is your chance to put your names on the Pawnee unity concert, the biggest event of 2014... besides the unveiling of Tricia's new highlights.
jokes.setup: Building up the concert as the biggest event
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging Tricia's highlights are equally important
jokes.explanation: Good small-town humor and Leslie's attention to personal details about everyone

jokes.text: You think I wouldn't notice? You look fly as hell, girl!
jokes.setup: Leslie commenting on Tricia's highlights
jokes.punchline: Her enthusiastic validation using outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Standard Leslie enthusiasm moment - caring about everyone's personal details

jokes.text: And dork-asaurus over here is gonna handle the boring stuff. / You said you genuinely valued my business acumen. / Also, I'm your boss.
jokes.setup: Tom calling Ben a 'dork-asaurus' and dismissing him
jokes.punchline: Ben's deadpan reminder of their actual relationship
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic between Tom's arrogance and Ben's practical authority, delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: It says 'Property of Pawnee government.' Isn't that adorable?
jokes.setup: Leslie showing off a baby gift
jokes.punchline: The bureaucratic label being treated as cute
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - finding government bureaucracy adorable and treating it as a sweet gesture

jokes.text: The kool-aid guy makes it look so easy.
jokes.setup: Tom attempting to break through a wall and failing
jokes.punchline: His comparison to the Kool-Aid mascot
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference applied to physical comedy failure, relatable and well-timed

jokes.text: I've taken great pains to see as little of this building as possible. Turns out the third floor is silent, empty, and completely free of government work.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his discovery of the third floor
jokes.punchline: His ideal workplace is one without any work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - finding paradise in the absence of government activity, delivered with characteristic deadpan

jokes.text: pearlies for girlies. / It's unisex. / The cartoon princess on the label says so.
jokes.setup: Product being marketed as unisex
jokes.punchline: The evidence being a cartoon princess
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist marketing logic - using feminine imagery to prove gender neutrality

jokes.text: I may have a few eggs ready to hatch in the personal idea nest.
jokes.setup: Tom being asked about business ideas
jokes.punchline: His overly elaborate metaphor for having ideas
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom over-complicated way of saying simple things, characteristic but not exceptional

jokes.text: Bees! Bees! Oh! Nobody panic! Ohhh, noooo! Ah! It stung me in the eyeball!
jokes.setup: Wall breaking ceremony
jokes.punchline: Chaos erupting from unexpected bees
jokes.explanation: Strong physical comedy moment with good escalation from ceremony to chaos, high impact

jokes.text: The stupid Eagletonians are totally getting the pwned by these bees. I'm gonna send this straight to tosh.
jokes.setup: Eagletonians getting stung by bees
jokes.punchline: Pawnee resident's petty celebration and internet culture reference
jokes.explanation: Good representation of petty town rivalry and social media culture, solid character moment

jokes.text: Don't worry, babe, I'll protect you. I got stung once, I'm immune. Go ahead and sting me, bees. It does nothing.
jokes.setup: Man protecting girlfriend from bees
jokes.punchline: His false bravado about bee immunity
jokes.explanation: Standard masculine bravado humor, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Nice try, prank queen. Probably bees in there.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to convince reporter it wasn't a prank
jokes.punchline: Reporter's continued insistence it was planned
jokes.explanation: Builds the running gag of everyone thinking Leslie planned the bee attack

jokes.text: Plus, one Pawneean was stung... in his mouth because he was laughing at the Eagletonians.
jokes.setup: Leslie defending herself from bee prank accusations
jokes.punchline: Revealing poetic justice of the one Pawnee victim
jokes.explanation: Excellent karmic justice detail. The mouth sting for laughing is perfect poetic punishment.

jokes.text: Don't mention my bee hole.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to avoid topic with Grant Larson
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Cheap innuendo humor but with good economy. Leslie's obliviousness sells it somewhat.

jokes.text: My son is several weeks old. He's quite familiar with the sound of power tools.
jokes.setup: Construction worker warning about noise
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact response about his baby's tool experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron parenting - practical, masculine approach to child-rearing delivered deadpan

jokes.text: Lasik for fingernails? Cut your fingernails once, never have to cut 'em again.
jokes.setup: Tom pitching business ideas
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical application of laser surgery
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd idea that sounds like Tom - mixing high-tech solutions with unnecessary problems

jokes.text: 'Saltweens!' Saltines for tweens.
jokes.setup: Tom continuing to pitch ideas
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary demographic targeting of basic crackers
jokes.explanation: Good Tom idea - unnecessary rebranding of existing products for specific demographics

jokes.text: 'Saltweens!' Saltines for tweens.
jokes.setup: Another Tom business pitch
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily specific demographic targeting for crackers
jokes.explanation: Good Tom-style unnecessary market segmentation. The portmanteau is clever if pointless.

jokes.text: You can't lean in to whisper and then yell. That's not fair.
jokes.setup: Reporter doing dramatic whisper-to-yell transition
jokes.punchline: April's practical complaint about the technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - logical complaint about illogical behavior, delivered with characteristic directness

jokes.text: No, I'm not. But I do have an idea.
jokes.setup: Ben saying Tom is 52 years old
jokes.punchline: Tom correcting the age but not the lack of ideas part
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - focusing on the less important part of the insult while acknowledging the main point.

jokes.text: You can't lean in to whisper and then yell. That's not fair.
jokes.setup: Reporter's dramatic whisper-to-yell delivery
jokes.punchline: April calling out the unfair dramatic technique
jokes.explanation: Great April observation about media manipulation tactics. Her deadpan analysis of dramatic techniques is perfect.

jokes.text: I got all help I'll need right here. [referring to baby]
jokes.setup: Construction crew questioning if Ron can do all the work himself
jokes.punchline: Ron indicating his weeks-old baby is his construction partner
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - Ron's complete confidence in his infant son as construction help

jokes.text: This is so boring! This is like listening to a TED talk by the color beige.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining boring business details
jokes.punchline: Tom's creative metaphor for ultimate boredom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, specific metaphor that perfectly captures extreme boredom in an unexpected, quotable way

jokes.text: Excuse me, Mike. That's, uh, best friend.
jokes.setup: Reporter calling Jamm Leslie's friend
jokes.punchline: Jamm's needy correction to 'best friend'
jokes.explanation: Good Jamm character moment - his desperate need for validation and friendship with Leslie

jokes.text: Got a lotta filth in my kindle anyway.
jokes.setup: Jamm talking about dirty magazines
jokes.punchline: Revealing he has digital porn collection
jokes.explanation: More Jamm crudeness. The Kindle detail is mildly amusing but overall fairly cheap humor.

jokes.text: Wish I had my ninja stars!
jokes.setup: Hospital fight escalating
jokes.punchline: Jamm wishing for completely inappropriate weapons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm absurdity - treating hospital argument like ninja battle. Great economy and character-specific weirdness.

jokes.text: I just wanted you to have a brief moment of happiness before you got sadder.
jokes.setup: April claiming she had good news
jokes.punchline: Admitting she just wanted to give false hope before delivering bad news
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - her dark, honest approach to human psychology delivered with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: It's to distract people from my black eye. See? It's working.
jokes.setup: Leslie wearing tie-dye shirt
jokes.punchline: Her logic that bright shirt distracts from injury
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie logic - her attempt at damage control through fashion choices. Visual comedy enhanced by her confidence.

jokes.text: It's just like a big, fun... stinking turd nugget. It sucks.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to stay positive about challenges
jokes.punchline: Her optimism completely breaking down mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's facade breaking - her attempt at positivity crumbling into crude honesty

jokes.text: Ben! You brought the wrong easel. God! Can't take this guy anywhere.
jokes.setup: Tom's presentation showing wrong information
jokes.punchline: Blaming Ben for his own mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom move - deflecting blame for his own preparation failure onto Ben

jokes.text: Liam Bonneville actually used it to humiliate staffers, but... he's kind of a bad boy.
jokes.setup: Grant explaining what happened to Leslie's proposal
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of workplace humiliation because he's a 'bad boy'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke while showing bureaucratic tolerance for abuse if someone has a reputation

jokes.text: your boss here sure knows his stuff.
jokes.setup: Tom claiming Ben is his employee throughout the meeting
jokes.punchline: Investor complimenting 'employee' Ben's expertise
jokes.explanation: Good ironic payoff - the 'employee' being more impressive than the 'boss' to the investor

jokes.text: This subpar toolbox only had one wrench, so I was lucky it was a three-quarters or I'd have been screwed.
jokes.setup: Ron telling detailed radiator repair story
jokes.punchline: The pun on 'screwed' in context of actual screws
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that works naturally in Ron's technical explanation, though puns are generally lower craft

jokes.text: You like fixing this town, Leslie, you always have. You know it's an uphill battle, but you love the struggle.
jokes.setup: Ron's radiator metaphor
jokes.punchline: His surprisingly insightful analysis of Leslie's psychology
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Ron showing unexpected emotional intelligence through practical metaphor

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I meant... because I can think of a lot of reasons not to take it.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying she can't think of one reason not to take the job
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifying she meant the opposite
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Leslie's conflicted feelings about leaving Pawnee

jokes.text: That's not your hand. Here it is. / Sorry, my depth perception is totally screwed up.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to shake hands
jokes.punchline: Missing because of her black eye affecting vision
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier injury with physical comedy payoff

jokes.text: That's not your hand. Here it is. Sorry, my depth perception is totally screwed up.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to shake hands
jokes.punchline: Missing Grant's hand due to her black eye
jokes.explanation: Straightforward physical comedy callback to her injury. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: So as of now, you own zero shares.
jokes.setup: Tom accepting Ben as second investor
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifying Ben gets nothing for free
jokes.explanation: Good Tom business attitude - appreciating support but not giving away actual value

jokes.text: Most people in this world, John, are ass[bleep].
jokes.setup: Ron teaching his baby about life
jokes.punchline: His cynical life lesson to an infant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron parenting philosophy - sharing his worldview with inappropriate honesty to his newborn

jokes.text: He now has that satisfied look that only comes with the pride of labor. Or he pooped. Either way, well done, John.
jokes.setup: Ron interpreting his baby's satisfied expression as pride in work
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging it might just be pooping, but being proud either way
jokes.explanation: Excellent ending joke - Ron's philosophical approach to parenting with perfect misdirection and character consistency
